{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_5044cca93bfd4810b381cbba","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Upload & Retrieval Pattern Test Matrix\n2\t\n3\t**Date:** 2026-07-15 · **Companion to:** `adr/002-multipart-corruption-detection-gaps.md`\n4\t\n5\tARMOR fronts several very different S3 clients, and the 40-day multipart corruption incident (ADR-002) proved that \"the tests pass\" means nothing unless the tests exercise **the same code paths real clients hit, at the same layer the bug can live in**. The original bug was in HTTP *routing* — so backend-level tests and the 1KB canary were structurally blind to it. This matrix enumerates every upload/retrieval pattern a real consumer uses, where it is (or isn't) tested, and at which layer.\n6\t\n7\t## Layers\n8\t\n9\t| Layer | What it can catch | What it cannot |\n10\t|---|---|---|\n11\t| **L1 unit** (e.g. `aws_chunked_test.go`) | encoding/parsing logic | routing, crypto interplay, B2 semantics |\n12\t| **L2 backend** (`internal/backend/*_test.go`, mock store) | part assembly logic, backend API contract | HTTP routing (where the ADR-002 bug lived), encryption |\n13\t| **L3 HTTP handler** (`internal/server/handlers/*_test.go`, httptest + mock backend) | routing, encryption/decryption round-trip, S3 XML/headers | real B2 behavior, network |\n14\t| **L4 real B2** (canary in prod; `armor-decrypt` offline) | end-to-end truth incl. B2 assembly | only what it's pointed at |\n15\t\n16\t**Rule from ADR-002:** every *write* pattern needs coverage at L3 (routing + crypto) **and** L4 (canary or drill); every *read* pattern needs L3 at minimum.\n17\t\n18\t## Real consumers and the patterns they use\n19\t\n20\t| Consumer | Upload patterns | Retrieval patterns |\n21\t|---|---|---|\n22\t| **litestream** (queue-api SQLite backups — the ADR-002 victim) | small WAL-segment PUTs; **multipart snapshots >5MiB**; aws-chunked streaming bodies | full GET on restore (the failure that exposed the incident) |\n23\t| **barman** (Postgres WAL, iad-native-ads incident) | steady small/medium PUTs; multipart base backups | full GET on recovery |\n24\t| **clone-workers / commitgraph** (parquet corpus — the load-bearing dataset) | **large multipart PUTs** via SDK defaults (5–16MiB parts, possibly parallel) | — |\n25\t| **duckdb / pyiceberg / website-builder** (corpus readers) | — | **parquet access pattern: suffix-range GET (footer), then many small interior range GETs**; HEAD; List |\n26\t| **Canary** (in-cluster, every 5min + hourly) | 1KB single PUT; multipart canary (ADR-002 §1) | GET + byte/HMAC/SHA verify |\n27\t| **armor-decrypt** (offline DR tool) | — | direct-from-B2 read + decrypt, bypassing the proxy |\n28\t| **Cloudflare zero-egress delivery** | — | GET (+Range) through CF CDN in front of ARMOR |\n29\t\n30\t## Upload patterns\n31\t\n32\t| # | Pattern | Real client | Coverage | Status |\n33\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n34\t| U1 | Single PUT, small (<64KiB block) | litestream WAL, state files | L3 `TestPutObjectGetObject`, `TestEncryptionRoundTrip`; L4 5-min canary | ✅ |\n35\t| U2 | Single PUT, large (streaming encryption path, ≥threshold) | any client below its multipart cutover | L3 `TestStreamingEncryptionLargeFile`/`MultiBlock`/`Threshold`/`SHA256` | ✅ |\n36\t| U3 | **aws-chunked streaming PUT** (`STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD`) | AWS SDKs, litestream (default for streamed bodies) | L1 `aws_chunked_test.go` (**added 2026-07-15**, unrunnable until `bf-15sdaf` fixes the package build); no L3 test sends a chunked-encoded body end-to-end | ⚠️ L1 pending build fix — L3 gap |\n37\t| U4 | **Multipart, standard S3 flow** (POST `?uploads` → PUT `?partNumber&uploadId` → POST `?uploadId`) — **the ADR-002 bug path** | litestream snapshots, barman base backups, SDK parquet uploads | L3 routing tripwire `TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut` (**passing**) + full-cycle byte verify `TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification` (**added 2026-07-15 — immediately found `bf-24sxh7` and is skip-gated on it**); L2 byte-verify suite (`multipart_integration_test.go`); L4 hourly multipart canary | 🔴 write path ✅, **read-back broken** (`bf-24sxh7`) |\n38\t| U5 | Multipart with POST-style part upload (non-standard; the only path pre-0.1.35 ARMOR handled) | rare | routed (`handlers.go` POST branch); no dedicated test | ⚠️ low-risk gap |\n39\t| U6 | **Out-of-order / parallel part upload** | **boto3/SDK default: parts upload concurrently** | none — see `TestMultipartSuspectPatterns` (skipped, documents why) | 🔴 **SUSPECT**: `UploadPart` derives the CTR counter from *arrival-order* cumulative `EncryptedBytes` (`handlers.go:1992`); parts arriving out of order get wrong counter offsets → silent corruption by design, not by regression |\n40\t| U7 | **Part retry** (same partNumber re-uploaded after network failure) | all SDKs retry parts | none | 🔴 **SUSPECT**: retried part increments `EncryptedBytes` again → all subsequent counters shift |\n41\t| U8 | **Non-block-aligned intermediate parts** (e.g. 10,000,000-byte parts; anything not a multiple of 64KiB) | configurable in every SDK | none | 🔴 **SUSPECT**: `startBlockIndex = EncryptedBytes / BlockSize` truncates on unaligned boundaries |\n42\t| U9 | Single-part multipart (multipart flow, 1 part) | SDKs near threshold | L2 `TestMultipartUploadSinglePart` | ✅ L2 (L3 covered implicitly by U4 cycle) |\n43\t| U10 | Abort + orphan cleanup | any failed upload | L3 `TestAbortMultipartUpload`(+`NotFound`) | ✅ |\n44\t| U11 | Presigned PUT/GET (`internal/presign`) | dashboard/browser flows | unit tests in `internal/presign` | ⚠️ verify scope |\n45\t| U12 | Server-side copy (incl. DEK rewrap) | metadata updates (multipart complete uses it) | L3 `TestCopyObject*` | ✅ |\n46\t\n47\t## Retrieval patterns\n48\t\n49\t| # | Pattern | Real client | Coverage | Status |\n50\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n51\t| R1 | Full GET + decrypt (small & streaming) | restores, readers | L3 `TestPutObjectGetObject`, `TestStreamingDecryption(VariousSizes)` | ✅ |\n52\t| R2 | Bounded range GET (`bytes=a-b`) | duckdb/pyiceberg column chunks | L3 `TestGetObjectRange`, `TestStreamingEncryptionRangeRead` | ✅ |\n53\t| R3 | Suffix range GET (`bytes=-N`) | **parquet footer read — first op of every corpus query** | L3 `TestRangeSuffixRequest` | ✅ |\n54\t| R4 | **Range GET on a multipart-assembled object** | parquet readers on corpus files (all >5MiB → all multipart) | `TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification` range + suffix-range steps (**added 2026-07-15**, skip-gated on `bf-24sxh7`) | 🔴 **broken** (`bf-24sxh7`) |\n55\t| R5 | HEAD (incl. manifest fast path) | readers, sync tools | L3 `TestHeadObject`, `TestHeadObjectManifest*` | ✅ |\n56\t| R6 | Conditional GET/HEAD (If-Match/None-Match, ±Range) | CDN revalidation | L3 `TestConditionalRequests*`, `TestConditionalRequestsWithRange` | ✅ |\n57\t| R7 | Read-after-complete (immediately GET what multipart just assembled) | litestream restore verification | new U4 full-cycle test (L3, skip-gated on `bf-24sxh7`); hourly multipart canary (L4) | 🔴 **broken** (`bf-24sxh7`) |\n58\t| R8 | GET (+Range) **through the Cloudflare zero-egress path** | all public delivery | none automated — prod-only | ⚠️ ops gap: add a scheduled external probe |\n59\t| R9 | Offline decrypt from raw B2 (`armor-decrypt`) — proxy-bypass truth check | DR drills | manual only | ⚠️ should be part of any pre-cutover drill |\n60\t| R10 | List/ListV2/versions/prefix (Hive partition keys) | corpus discovery, pyiceberg | L3 `TestListObjectsV2`, `TestListObjectVersions`, `TestURLDecodeHivePartitionKeys`, cache tests | ✅ |\n61\t\n62\t## Known integrity gaps (found while building this matrix, 2026-07-15) — beads filed\n63\t\n64\t1. **`bf-24sxh7` (P0): multipart objects are unreadable through ARMOR's GET.** The read path (`handleFullObjectStream`, `handlers.go:720-727`, and the range path) assumes the single-PUT layout — 64-byte header + data + embedded HMAC table — and never checks the `armor-multipart` metadata flag. Multipart objects are raw concatenated part ciphertext with the HMAC table in a *sidecar*, so every GET 500s (\"Failed to prefetch HMAC table: offset out of range\"). Found by the new full-cycle test on its first run. Likely a second contributor to the ADR-002 \"snapshot didn't decode\" incident, independent of the routing bug.\n65\t2. **`bf-1v2ehf` (P2): multipart objects store a placeholder plaintext-SHA.** `CompleteMultipartUpload` sets `plaintextSHA = sha256(\"\")` (`handlers.go:2132-2134`) — the manifest/provenance SHA for every multipart object is the empty hash; real corruption can't be distinguished from the placeholder.\n66\t3. **`bf-59unr3` (P1): U6/U7/U8 are design-level suspects, not regression risks.** The per-part CTR offset scheme assumes strictly sequential, exactly-once, block-aligned parts. Real SDKs violate all three by default. Until fixed or explicitly rejected (400 rather than corrupt), **clients writing through ARMOR must be pinned to sequential, block-aligned, no-retry part behavior — including the commitgraph clone-workers.**\n67\t4. **`bf-15sdaf` (P1): `internal/server` does not compile on main** (redeclared helpers from the recent bead-thrash commits), blocking the whole-module build and the new L1 chunked-decoder test.\n68\t5. Consumers on pre-fix ARMOR versions: per ADR-002, `iad-ci` (0.1.24) and `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager` (0.1.13) remain on multipart-corrupting builds. `ord-devimprint` is patched (0.1.42, verified 2026-07-15).\n69\t\n70\t**Consequence for the commitgraph rebuild:** until `bf-24sxh7` and `bf-59unr3` are fixed *and* the multipart canary is deployed to the target cluster, ARMOR multipart cannot be certified for the corpus write path. Interim options: single-PUT streaming writes only (stay under the SDK multipart cutover), or direct-B2 writes with ARMOR reserved for delivery.\n71\t\n72\t## Test-run cheat sheet\n73\t\n74\t```\n75\tgo test ./internal/server/...          # L1 + L3 (routing tripwire, full-cycle, chunked decode)\n76\tgo test ./internal/backend/...         # L2 multipart assembly byte-verification\n77\tcurl $ARMOR/armor/canary               # L4: small + multipart canary status in prod\n78\t```\n79\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5c8de2f8-d1ee-475e-9caa-f72cce08f189","uuid":"bc7447c6-e623-4d72-a834-d0a987abd26f","timestamp":"2026-07-15T12:19:08.768Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md","content":"# Upload & Retrieval Pattern Test Matrix\n\n**Date:** 2026-07-15 · **Companion to:** `adr/002-multipart-corruption-detection-gaps.md`\n\nARMOR fronts several very different S3 clients, and the 40-day multipart corruption incident (ADR-002) proved that \"the tests pass\" means nothing unless the tests exercise **the same code paths real clients hit, at the same layer the bug can live in**. The original bug was in HTTP *routing* — so backend-level tests and the 1KB canary were structurally blind to it. This matrix enumerates every upload/retrieval pattern a real consumer uses, where it is (or isn't) tested, and at which layer.\n\n## Layers\n\n| Layer | What it can catch | What it cannot |\n|---|---|---|\n| **L1 unit** (e.g. `aws_chunked_test.go`) | encoding/parsing logic | routing, crypto interplay, B2 semantics |\n| **L2 backend** (`internal/backend/*_test.go`, mock store) | part assembly logic, backend API contract | HTTP routing (where the ADR-002 bug lived), encryption |\n| **L3 HTTP handler** (`internal/server/handlers/*_test.go`, httptest + mock backend) | routing, encryption/decryption round-trip, S3 XML/headers | real B2 behavior, network |\n| **L4 real B2** (canary in prod; `armor-decrypt` offline) | end-to-end truth incl. B2 assembly | only what it's pointed at |\n\n**Rule from ADR-002:** every *write* pattern needs coverage at L3 (routing + crypto) **and** L4 (canary or drill); every *read* pattern needs L3 at minimum.\n\n## Real consumers and the patterns they use\n\n| Consumer | Upload patterns | Retrieval patterns |\n|---|---|---|\n| **litestream** (queue-api SQLite backups — the ADR-002 victim) | small WAL-segment PUTs; **multipart snapshots >5MiB**; aws-chunked streaming bodies | full GET on restore (the failure that exposed the incident) |\n| **barman** (Postgres WAL, iad-native-ads incident) | steady small/medium PUTs; multipart base backups | full GET on recovery |\n| **clone-workers / commitgraph** (parquet corpus — the load-bearing dataset) | **large multipart PUTs** via SDK defaults (5–16MiB parts, possibly parallel) | — |\n| **duckdb / pyiceberg / website-builder** (corpus readers) | — | **parquet access pattern: suffix-range GET (footer), then many small interior range GETs**; HEAD; List |\n| **Canary** (in-cluster, every 5min + hourly) | 1KB single PUT; multipart canary (ADR-002 §1) | GET + byte/HMAC/SHA verify |\n| **armor-decrypt** (offline DR tool) | — | direct-from-B2 read + decrypt, bypassing the proxy |\n| **Cloudflare zero-egress delivery** | — | GET (+Range) through CF CDN in front of ARMOR |\n\n## Upload patterns\n\n| # | Pattern | Real client | Coverage | Status |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| U1 | Single PUT, small (<64KiB block) | litestream WAL, state files | L3 `TestPutObjectGetObject`, `TestEncryptionRoundTrip`; L4 5-min canary | ✅ |\n| U2 | Single PUT, large (streaming encryption path, ≥threshold) | any client below its multipart cutover | L3 `TestStreamingEncryptionLargeFile`/`MultiBlock`/`Threshold`/`SHA256` | ✅ |\n| U3 | **aws-chunked streaming PUT** (`STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD`) | AWS SDKs, litestream (default for streamed bodies) | L1 `aws_chunked_test.go` (**added 2026-07-15**, unrunnable until `bf-15sdaf` fixes the package build); no L3 test sends a chunked-encoded body end-to-end | ⚠️ L1 pending build fix — L3 gap |\n| U4 | **Multipart, standard S3 flow** (POST `?uploads` → PUT `?partNumber&uploadId` → POST `?uploadId`) — **the ADR-002 bug path** | litestream snapshots, barman base backups, SDK parquet uploads | L3 routing tripwire `TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut` (**passing**) + full-cycle byte verify `TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification` (**added 2026-07-15 — immediately found `bf-24sxh7` and is skip-gated on it**); L2 byte-verify suite (`multipart_integration_test.go`); L4 hourly multipart canary | 🔴 write path ✅, **read-back broken** (`bf-24sxh7`) |\n| U5 | Multipart with POST-style part upload (non-standard; the only path pre-0.1.35 ARMOR handled) | rare | routed (`handlers.go` POST branch); no dedicated test | ⚠️ low-risk gap |\n| U6 | **Out-of-order / parallel part upload** | **boto3/SDK default: parts upload concurrently** | none — see `TestMultipartSuspectPatterns` (skipped, documents why) | 🔴 **SUSPECT**: `UploadPart` derives the CTR counter from *arrival-order* cumulative `EncryptedBytes` (`handlers.go:1992`); parts arriving out of order get wrong counter offsets → silent corruption by design, not by regression |\n| U7 | **Part retry** (same partNumber re-uploaded after network failure) | all SDKs retry parts | none | 🔴 **SUSPECT**: retried part increments `EncryptedBytes` again → all subsequent counters shift |\n| U8 | **Non-block-aligned intermediate parts** (e.g. 10,000,000-byte parts; anything not a multiple of 64KiB) | configurable in every SDK | none | 🔴 **SUSPECT**: `startBlockIndex = EncryptedBytes / BlockSize` truncates on unaligned boundaries |\n| U9 | Single-part multipart (multipart flow, 1 part) | SDKs near threshold | L2 `TestMultipartUploadSinglePart` | ✅ L2 (L3 covered implicitly by U4 cycle) |\n| U10 | Abort + orphan cleanup | any failed upload | L3 `TestAbortMultipartUpload`(+`NotFound`) | ✅ |\n| U11 | Presigned PUT/GET (`internal/presign`) | dashboard/browser flows | unit tests in `internal/presign` | ⚠️ verify scope |\n| U12 | Server-side copy (incl. DEK rewrap) | metadata updates (multipart complete uses it) | L3 `TestCopyObject*` | ✅ |\n\n## Retrieval patterns\n\n| # | Pattern | Real client | Coverage | Status |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| R1 | Full GET + decrypt (small & streaming) | restores, readers | L3 `TestPutObjectGetObject`, `TestStreamingDecryption(VariousSizes)` | ✅ |\n| R2 | Bounded range GET (`bytes=a-b`) | duckdb/pyiceberg column chunks | L3 `TestGetObjectRange`, `TestStreamingEncryptionRangeRead` | ✅ |\n| R3 | Suffix range GET (`bytes=-N`) | **parquet footer read — first op of every corpus query** | L3 `TestRangeSuffixRequest` | ✅ |\n| R4 | **Range GET on a multipart-assembled object** | parquet readers on corpus files (all >5MiB → all multipart) | `TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification` range + suffix-range steps (**added 2026-07-15**, skip-gated on `bf-24sxh7`) | 🔴 **broken** (`bf-24sxh7`) |\n| R5 | HEAD (incl. manifest fast path) | readers, sync tools | L3 `TestHeadObject`, `TestHeadObjectManifest*` | ✅ |\n| R6 | Conditional GET/HEAD (If-Match/None-Match, ±Range) | CDN revalidation | L3 `TestConditionalRequests*`, `TestConditionalRequestsWithRange` | ✅ |\n| R7 | Read-after-complete (immediately GET what multipart just assembled) | litestream restore verification | new U4 full-cycle test (L3, skip-gated on `bf-24sxh7`); hourly multipart canary (L4) | 🔴 **broken** (`bf-24sxh7`) |\n| R8 | GET (+Range) **through the Cloudflare zero-egress path** | all public delivery | none automated — prod-only | ⚠️ ops gap: add a scheduled external probe |\n| R9 | Offline decrypt from raw B2 (`armor-decrypt`) — proxy-bypass truth check | DR drills | manual only | ⚠️ should be part of any pre-cutover drill |\n| R10 | List/ListV2/versions/prefix (Hive partition keys) | corpus discovery, pyiceberg | L3 `TestListObjectsV2`, `TestListObjectVersions`, `TestURLDecodeHivePartitionKeys`, cache tests | ✅ |\n\n## Known integrity gaps (found while building this matrix, 2026-07-15) — beads filed\n\n1. **`bf-24sxh7` (P0): multipart objects are unreadable through ARMOR's GET.** The read path (`handleFullObjectStream`, `handlers.go:720-727`, and the range path) assumes the single-PUT layout — 64-byte header + data + embedded HMAC table — and never checks the `armor-multipart` metadata flag. Multipart objects are raw concatenated part ciphertext with the HMAC table in a *sidecar*, so every GET 500s (\"Failed to prefetch HMAC table: offset out of range\"). Found by the new full-cycle test on its first run. Likely a second contributor to the ADR-002 \"snapshot didn't decode\" incident, independent of the routing bug.\n2. **`bf-1v2ehf` (P2): multipart objects store a placeholder plaintext-SHA.** `CompleteMultipartUpload` sets `plaintextSHA = sha256(\"\")` (`handlers.go:2132-2134`) — the manifest/provenance SHA for every multipart object is the empty hash; real corruption can't be distinguished from the placeholder.\n3. **`bf-59unr3` (P1): U6/U7/U8 are design-level suspects, not regression risks.** The per-part CTR offset scheme assumes strictly sequential, exactly-once, block-aligned parts. Real SDKs violate all three by default. Until fixed or explicitly rejected (400 rather than corrupt), **clients writing through ARMOR must be pinned to sequential, block-aligned, no-retry part behavior — including the commitgraph clone-workers.**\n4. **`bf-15sdaf` (P1): `internal/server` does not compile on main** (redeclared helpers from the recent bead-thrash commits), blocking the whole-module build and the new L1 chunked-decoder test.\n5. Consumers on pre-fix ARMOR versions: per ADR-002, `iad-ci` (0.1.24) and `iad-kalshi`/`rs-manager` (0.1.13) remain on multipart-corrupting builds. `ord-devimprint` is patched (0.1.42, verified 2026-07-15).\n\n**Consequence for the commitgraph rebuild:** until `bf-24sxh7` and `bf-59unr3` are fixed *and* the multipart canary is deployed to the target cluster, ARMOR multipart cannot be certified for the corpus write path. Interim options: single-PUT streaming writes only (stay under the SDK multipart cutover), or direct-B2 writes with ARMOR reserved for delivery.\n\n## Test-run cheat sheet\n\n```\ngo test ./internal/server/...          # L1 + L3 (routing tripwire, full-cycle, chunked decode)\ngo test ./internal/backend/...         # L2 multipart assembly byte-verification\ncurl $ARMOR/armor/canary               # L4: small + multipart canary status in prod\n```\n","numLines":79,"startLine":1,"totalLines":79}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_7d5596edb3e04adfb71a9db9","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage handlers_test\n2\t\n3\t// Tests for the multipart upload HTTP surface, added per ADR-002 and\n4\t// docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md (U4, R4, R7).\n5\t//\n6\t// The 2026-06 corruption bug lived in ROUTING: a standard client's\n7\t// `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` fell through to plain PutObject, storing each\n8\t// part as the whole object. These tests exercise the full HTTP handler path\n10\t// with a recording backend so a routing regression fails loudly.\n11\t\n12\timport (\n13\t\t\"bytes\"\n14\t\t\"context\"\n15\t\t\"crypto/rand\"\n16\t\t\"encoding/xml\"\n17\t\t\"fmt\"\n18\t\t\"io\"\n19\t\t\"net/http\"\n20\t\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n21\t\t\"strconv\"\n22\t\t\"sync\"\n23\t\t\"testing\"\n24\t\n25\t\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/backend\"\n26\t\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\"\n27\t\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/keymanager\"\n28\t\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/server/handlers\"\n29\t)\n30\t\n31\t// concatenated (in the part order\n34\t// given to CompleteMultipartUpload) into the object map on completion —\n35\t// mirroring what B2 does on b2_finish_large_file.\n36\ttype recordingBackend struct {\n37\t\t*mockBackend\n38\t\n39\t\trmu      sync.Mutex\n40\t\tputKeys  []string // keys passed to Put (includes state/sidecar writes)\n41\t\tpartKeys []string // keys passed to UploadPart\n42\t\tuploads  map[string]map[int32][]byte\n43\t\tnextID   int\n44\t}\n45\t\n46\tfunc newRecordingBackend() *recordingBackend {\n47\t\treturn &recordingBackend{\n48\t\t\tmockBackend: newMockBackend(),\n49\t\t\tuploads:     make(map[string]map[int32][]byte),\n50\t\t}\n51\t}\n52\t\n53\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) Put(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, body io.Reader, size int64, meta map[string]string) error {\n54\t\tr.rmu.Lock()\n55\t\tr.putKeys = append(r.putKeys, bucket+\"/\"+key)\n56\t\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n57\t\treturn r.mockBackend.Put(ctx, bucket, key, body, size, meta)\n58\t}\n59\t\n60\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n61\t\tr.rmu.Lock()\n62\t\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n63\t\tr.nextID++\n64\t\tid := fmt.Sprintf(\"upload-%d\", r.nextID)\n65\t\tr.uploads[id] = make(map[int32][]byte)\n66\t\treturn id, nil\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n70\t\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n71\t\tif err != nil {\n72\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n73\t\t}\n74\t\tr.rmu.Lock()\n75\t\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n76\t\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\n77\t\tif !ok {\n78\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"no such upload: %s\", uploadID)\n79\t\t}\n80\t\tparts[partNumber] = data\n81\t\tr.partKeys = append(r.partKeys, bucket+\"/\"+key)\n82\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-part-%d\", partNumber), nil\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, completed []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n86\t\tr.rmu.Lock()\n87\t\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\n88\t\tif !ok {\n89\t\t\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n90\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"no such upload: %s\", uploadID)\n91\t\t}\n92\t\tvar assembled []byte\n93\t\tfor _, p := range completed {\n94\t\t\tdata, ok := parts[p.PartNumber]\n95\t\t\tif !ok {\n96\t\t\t\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n97\t\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"missing part %d\", p.PartNumber)\n98\t\t\t}\n99\t\t\tassembled = r.rmu.Unlock()\n118\t\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\n119\t\treturn nil\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\t// objectPutCount returns how many times Put was called with exactly this key.\n123\tfunc (r final part non-aligned.\n249\t\t// Scaled to 44MB+ total to match the actual failing scale from bf-1v6skf\n250\t\t// (production litestream snapshot was 44,908,497 bytes and failed at block 256).\n251\t\tconst targetTotal = 45 * 1024 * 1024 // 45MB target (slightly above production failure)\n252\t\tpartSize := 5 * 1024 * 1024          // 5MB per part (S3 minimum, matches production)\n253\t\tfinalPartSize := targetTotal - (2 * partSize)\n254\t\tsizes := []int{partSize, partSize, finalPartSize}\n255\t\tvar plaintext []byte\n256\t\tvar parts [][]byte\n257\t\tfor p, size := range sizes {\n258\t\t\tpart := make([]byte, size)\n259\t\t\tfor i := range part {\n260\t\t\t\tswitch p {\n261\t\t\t\tcase 0:\n262\t\t\t\t\tpart[i] = byte(i & 0xFF)\n263\t\t\t\tcase 1:\n264\t\t\t\t\tpart[i] = 0xFF - byte(i&0xFF)\n265\t\t\t\tcase 2:\n266\t\t\t\t\tif i%2 == 0 {\n267\t\t\t\t\t\tpart[i] = 0xAA\n268\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n269\t\t\t\t\t\tpart[i] = 0x55\n270\t\t\t\t\t}\n271\t\t\t\t}\n272\t\t\t}\n273\t\t\tparts = append(parts, part)\n274\t\t\tplaintext = append(plaintext, part...)\n275\t\t}\n276\t\n277\t\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n278\t\tvar etags []string\n279\t\tfor i, part := range parts {\n280\t\t\tetags = append(etags, uploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, i+1, part))\n281\t\t}\n282\t\tcompleteMultipart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, etags)\n283\t\n284\t\t// Full GET: byte-for-byte.\n285\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n286\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n287\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n288\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n289\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GET after complete failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n290\t\t}\n291\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\n292\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"full GET content mismatch: got %d bytes, want %d; first divergence at %d\",\n293\t\t\t\tw.Body.Len(), len(plaintext), firstDivergence(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext))\n294\t\t}\n295\t\n296\t\t// Bounded Range GET spanning the part-1/part-2 boundary.\n297\t\tlo, hi := 2*block-500, 2*block+499\n298\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n299\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes=%d-%d\", lo, hi))\n300\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n301\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n302\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\n303\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"range GET failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n304\t\t}\n305\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext[lo:hi+1]) {\n306\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"range GET across part boundary mismatch (bytes=%d-%d)\", lo, hi)\n307\t\t}\n308\t\n309\t\t// Suffix Range GET — the parquet footer access pattern (matrix R3/R4).\n310\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n311\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", \"bytes=-1000\")\n312\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n313\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n314\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\n315\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"suffix range GET failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n316\t\t}\n317\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext[len(plaintext)-1000:]) {\n318\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"suffix range GET (parquet footer pattern) content mismatch\")\n319\t\t}\n320\t\n321\t\t// HEAD: plaintext length, not ciphertext length.\n322\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodHead, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n323\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n324\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n325\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n326\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"HEAD after complete failed: status %d\", w.Code)\n327\t\t}\n328\t\tif cl := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Length\"); cl != strconv.Itoa(len(plaintext)) {\n329\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"HEAD Content-Length = %s, want %d\", cl, len(plaintext))\n330\t\t}\n331\t\n332\t\t_ = rb\n333\t}\n334\t\n335\t// TestMultipartSuspectPatterns documents the three multipart upload patterns\n336\t// real SDKs use by default that ARMOR's per-part CTR-offset scheme does not\n337\t// support (docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md U6/U7/U8):\n338\t//\n339\t//\tU6 out-of-order / parallel part upload  (boto3 uploads parts concurrently)\n340\t//\tU7 part retry after network failure     (every SDK retries parts)\n341\t//\tU8 non-block-aligned intermediate parts (any part size % 65536 != 0)\n342\t//\n343\t// UploadPart derives each part's CTR counter from arrival-order cumulative\n344\t// EncryptedBytes (handlers.go:1992), so all three silently corrupt rather\n345\t// than erroring. Until the design is fixed (per-part counters derived from\n346\t// partNumber × known part size, or a hard 400 on unsupported patterns),\n347\t// this test stays skipped as an executable TODO. Unskipping it without a\n348\t// design fix will (correctly) fail.\n349\tfunc TestMultipartSuspectPatterns(t *testing.T) {\n350\t\tt.Skip(\"KNOWN DESIGN GAP bf-59unr3 (matrix U6/U7/U8): out-of-order, retried, and non-aligned parts corrupt silently; clients must be pinned to sequential aligned no-retry behavior until fixed\")\n351\t}\n352\t\n353\tfunc firstDivergence(a, b []byte) int {\n354\t\tn := len(a)\n355\t\tif len(b) < n {\n356\t\t\tn = len(b)\n357\t\t}\n358\t\tfor i := 0; i < n; i++ {\n359\t\t\tif a[i] != b[i] {\n360\t\t\t\treturn i\n361\t\t\t}\n362\t\t}\n363\t\treturn n\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t// TestMultipartPartBoundaryDebug tests decryption at the exact part boundary.\n367\tfunc TestMultipartPartBoundaryDebug(t *testing.T) {\n368\t\t_, rb, h := handlers_test\n\n// Tests for the multipart upload HTTP surface, added per ADR-002 and\n// docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md (U4, R4, R7).\n//\n// The 2026-06 corruption bug lived in ROUTING: a standard client's\n// `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` fell through to plain PutObject, storing each\n// part as the whole object. Backend-level tests and the 1KB canary were\n// structurally blind to it. These tests exercise the full HTTP handler path\n// with a recording backend so a routing regression fails loudly.\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/rand\"\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/backend\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/keymanager\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/server/handlers\"\n)\n\n// recordingBackend wraps mockBackend, records which backend methods are\n// invoked per object key, and implements a real in-memory multipart\n// store: parts are kept per uploadID and concatenated (in the part order\n// given to CompleteMultipartUpload) into the object map on completion —\n// mirroring what B2 does on b2_finish_large_file.\ntype recordingBackend struct {\n\t*mockBackend\n\n\trmu      sync.Mutex\n\tputKeys  []string // keys passed to Put (includes state/sidecar writes)\n\tpartKeys []string // keys passed to UploadPart\n\tuploads  map[string]map[int32][]byte\n\tnextID   int\n}\n\nfunc newRecordingBackend() *recordingBackend {\n\treturn &recordingBackend{\n\t\tmockBackend: newMockBackend(),\n\t\tuploads:     make(map[string]map[int32][]byte),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) Put(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, body io.Reader, size int64, meta map[string]string) error {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tr.putKeys = append(r.putKeys, bucket+\"/\"+key)\n\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n\treturn r.mockBackend.Put(ctx, bucket, key, body, size, meta)\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n\tr.nextID++\n\tid := fmt.Sprintf(\"upload-%d\", r.nextID)\n\tr.uploads[id] = make(map[int32][]byte)\n\treturn id, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"no such upload: %s\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tparts[partNumber] = data\n\tr.partKeys = append(r.partKeys, bucket+\"/\"+key)\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-part-%d\", partNumber), nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, completed []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"no such upload: %s\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\tvar assembled []byte\n\tfor _, p := range completed {\n\t\tdata, ok := parts[p.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"missing part %d\", p.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tassembled = append(assembled, data...)\n\t}\n\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\n\tr.rmu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Store assembled object exactly as B2 would, preserving no metadata —\n\t// the handler applies ARMOR metadata afterwards via Copy.\n\tr.mockBackend.mu.Lock()\n\tr.mockBackend.objects[bucket+\"/\"+key] = assembled\n\tif _, ok := r.mockBackend.meta[bucket+\"/\"+key]; !ok {\n\t\tr.mockBackend.meta[bucket+\"/\"+key] = map[string]string{}\n\t}\n\tr.mockBackend.mu.Unlock()\n\treturn \"etag-assembled\", nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// objectPutCount returns how many times Put was called with exactly this key.\nfunc (r *recordingBackend) objectPutCount(bucket, key string) int {\n\tr.rmu.Lock()\n\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\n\tn := 0\n\tfor _, k := range r.putKeys {\n\t\tif k == bucket+\"/\"+key {\n\t\t\tn++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn n\n}\n\nfunc recordingTestSetup(t *testing.T) (*config.Config, *recordingBackend, *handlers.Handlers) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(mek); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate MEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n\t\tAuthAccessKey: \"test-access-key\",\n\t\tAuthSecretKey: \"test-secret-key\",\n\t}\n\trb := newRecordingBackend()\n\tcache := backend.NewMetadataCache(1000, 300)\n\tfooterCache := backend.NewFooterCache(1000, 300)\n\tkm, err := keymanager.New(mek, nil, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create key manager: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\th := handlers.New(cfg, rb, cache, footerCache, km, nil)\n\treturn cfg, rb, h\n}\n\nfunc initiateMultipart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key string) string {\n\tt.Helper()\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?uploads\", bucket, key), nil)\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/octet-stream\")\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t}\n\tvar result struct {\n\t\tUploadID string `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n\t}\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &result); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to parse initiate response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif result.UploadID == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"empty UploadId in initiate response\")\n\t}\n\treturn result.UploadID\n}\n\nfunc uploadPart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int, body []byte) string {\n\tt.Helper()\n\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?partNumber=%d&uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, partNumber, uploadID)\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(body))\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"UploadPart %d failed: status %d: %s\", partNumber, w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t}\n\tetag := w.Header().Get(\"ETag\")\n\tif etag == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"UploadPart %d returned no ETag\", partNumber)\n\t}\n\treturn etag\n}\n\nfunc completeMultipart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key, uploadID string, etags []string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\tvar xmlBody bytes.Buffer\n\txmlBody.WriteString(\"<CompleteMultipartUpload>\")\n\tfor i, etag := range etags {\n\t\txmlBody.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"<Part><PartNumber>%d</PartNumber><ETag>%s</ETag></Part>\", i+1, etag))\n\t}\n\txmlBody.WriteString(\"</CompleteMultipartUpload>\")\n\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s?uploadId=%s\", bucket, key, uploadID)\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, url, &xmlBody)\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut is the ADR-002 regression\n// tripwire. The 2026-06 bug routed `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` to PutObject;\n// this asserts the part request reaches UploadPart and that Put is never\n// invoked for the object key itself (state/sidecar writes use other keys).\nfunc TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut(t *testing.T) {\n\t_, rb, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"routing-tripwire.dat\"\n\n\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\tpart := make([]byte, 2*65536) // two full blocks\n\tfor i := range part {\n\t\tpart[i] = byte(i % 251)\n\t}\n\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part)\n\n\trb.rmu.Lock()\n\tpartCalls := len(rb.partKeys)\n\trb.rmu.Unlock()\n\tif partCalls == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"ROUTING REGRESSION: PUT ?partNumber&uploadId did not reach backend.UploadPart\")\n\t}\n\tif n := rb.objectPutCount(bucket, key); n != 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"ROUTING REGRESSION: backend.Put was called %d time(s) with the object key during a part upload — parts are being stored as whole objects (the ADR-002 corruption bug)\", n)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification uploads three distinguishable parts\n// (block-aligned intermediates + a non-aligned final part) through the real\n// HTTP handler path (initiate → parts → complete), then verifies retrieval\n// byte-for-byte via full GET, bounded Range GET across a part boundary,\n// suffix Range GET (the parquet footer pattern), and HEAD length.\n// Covers matrix rows U4, U9, R4, R7.\nfunc TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification(t *testing.T) {\n\t_, rb, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"full-cycle.parquet\"\n\tconst block = 65536\n\n\t// Three parts with distinguishable patterns; final part non-aligned.\n\t// Scaled to 44MB+ total to match the actual failing scale from bf-1v6skf\n\t// (production litestream snapshot was 44,908,497 bytes and failed at block 256).\n\tconst targetTotal = 45 * 1024 * 1024 // 45MB target (slightly above production failure)\n\tpartSize := 5 * 1024 * 1024          // 5MB per part (S3 minimum, matches production)\n\tfinalPartSize := targetTotal - (2 * partSize)\n\tsizes := []int{partSize, partSize, finalPartSize}\n\tvar plaintext []byte\n\tvar parts [][]byte\n\tfor p, size := range sizes {\n\t\tpart := make([]byte, size)\n\t\tfor i := range part {\n\t\t\tswitch p {\n\t\t\tcase 0:\n\t\t\t\tpart[i] = byte(i & 0xFF)\n\t\t\tcase 1:\n\t\t\t\tpart[i] = 0xFF - byte(i&0xFF)\n\t\t\tcase 2:\n\t\t\t\tif i%2 == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tpart[i] = 0xAA\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tpart[i] = 0x55\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tparts = append(parts, part)\n\t\tplaintext = append(plaintext, part...)\n\t}\n\n\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\tvar etags []string\n\tfor i, part := range parts {\n\t\tetags = append(etags, uploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, i+1, part))\n\t}\n\tcompleteMultipart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, etags)\n\n\t// Full GET: byte-for-byte.\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GET after complete failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t}\n\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"full GET content mismatch: got %d bytes, want %d; first divergence at %d\",\n\t\t\tw.Body.Len(), len(plaintext), firstDivergence(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext))\n\t}\n\n\t// Bounded Range GET spanning the part-1/part-2 boundary.\n\tlo, hi := 2*block-500, 2*block+499\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes=%d-%d\", lo, hi))\n\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"range GET failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t}\n\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext[lo:hi+1]) {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"range GET across part boundary mismatch (bytes=%d-%d)\", lo, hi)\n\t}\n\n\t// Suffix Range GET — the parquet footer access pattern (matrix R3/R4).\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", \"bytes=-1000\")\n\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"suffix range GET failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t}\n\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext[len(plaintext)-1000:]) {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"suffix range GET (parquet footer pattern) content mismatch\")\n\t}\n\n\t// HEAD: plaintext length, not ciphertext length.\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodHead, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"HEAD after complete failed: status %d\", w.Code)\n\t}\n\tif cl := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Length\"); cl != strconv.Itoa(len(plaintext)) {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"HEAD Content-Length = %s, want %d\", cl, len(plaintext))\n\t}\n\n\t_ = rb\n}\n\n// TestMultipartSuspectPatterns documents the three multipart upload patterns\n// real SDKs use by default that ARMOR's per-part CTR-offset scheme does not\n// support (docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md U6/U7/U8):\n//\n//\tU6 out-of-order / parallel part upload  (boto3 uploads parts concurrently)\n//\tU7 part retry after network failure     (every SDK retries parts)\n//\tU8 non-block-aligned intermediate parts (any part size % 65536 != 0)\n//\n// UploadPart derives each part's CTR counter from arrival-order cumulative\n// EncryptedBytes (handlers.go:1992), so all three silently corrupt rather\n// than erroring. Until the design is fixed (per-part counters derived from\n// partNumber × known part size, or a hard 400 on unsupported patterns),\n// this test stays skipped as an executable TODO. Unskipping it without a\n// design fix will (correctly) fail.\nfunc TestMultipartSuspectPatterns(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Skip(\"KNOWN DESIGN GAP bf-59unr3 (matrix U6/U7/U8): out-of-order, retried, and non-aligned parts corrupt silently; clients must be pinned to sequential aligned no-retry behavior until fixed\")\n}\n\nfunc firstDivergence(a, b []byte) int {\n\tn := len(a)\n\tif len(b) < n {\n\t\tn = len(b)\n\t}\n\tfor i := 0; i < n; i++ {\n\t\tif a[i] != b[i] {\n\t\t\treturn i\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn n\n}\n\n// TestMultipartPartBoundaryDebug tests decryption at the exact part boundary.\nfunc TestMultipartPartBoundaryDebug(t *testing.T) {\n\t_, rb, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\n\tbucket, key := \"test-bucket\", \"boundary-debug.parquet\"\n\tconst block = 65536\n\tconst partSize = 5 * 1024 * 1024\n\n\t// Two simple parts\n\tpart1 := make([]byte, partSize)\n\tpart2 := make([]byte, partSize)\n\tfor i := range part1 {\n\t\tpart1[i] = 0xAA\n\t\tpart2[i] = 0xBB\n\t}\n\n\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\n\tetag1 := uploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part1)\n\tetag2 := uploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 2, part2)\n\tcompleteMultipart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, []string{etag1, etag2})\n\n\t// Check last 10 bytes of part 1\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", \"bytes=5242870-5242879\")\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"range GET failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t}\n\tpart1End := w.Body.Bytes()\n\tt.Logf(\"Part 1 end (bytes 5242870-5242879): %v\", part1End)\n\n\t// Check first 10 bytes of part 2\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/\"+bucket+\"/\"+key, nil)\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", \"bytes=5242880-5242889\")\n\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"range GET failed: status %d: %s\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\n\t}\n\tpart2Start := w.Body.Bytes()\n\tt.Logf(\"Part 2 start (bytes 5242880-5242889): %v\", part2Start)\n\n\t// Verify part 1 end is all 0xAA\n\tfor _, b := range part1End {\n\t\tif b != 0xAA {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Part 1 end has wrong byte: got 0x%02x, want 0xAA\", b)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify part 2 start is all 0xBB\n\tfor i, b := range part2Start {\n\t\tif b != 0xBB {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Part 2 start byte %d is wrong: got 0x%02x, want 0xBB\", i, b)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t_ = rb\n}\n","numLines":424,"startLine":1,"totalLines":424}}}
